answer:
\boxed{G}

The **development and application of steam-powered technologies** (G) occurred well after the period of Western European global hegemony (1450–1750 C.E.), as steam power became significant only in the late 18th and early 19th centuries. The other options—such as joint-stock companies, colonial labor exploitation, naval advancements, and gunpowder—directly enabled European expansion, trade dominance, and military superiority during this era. Thus, G is the least relevant factor.
